name: Sant Pere de Vilanoveta from Aramunt — circular through the Bosque de Sant Corneli
name_original: Sant Pere de Vilanoveta desde Aramunt — circular por el Bosque de Sant Corneli
region_intro: Hiking around Conca De Dalt offers diverse experiences within the Lleida Pre-Pyrenees region of Catalonia. The area features a mountainous environment with significant elevation changes, pronounced ravines, and dramatic cliffs, including the Collegats Canyon. The landscape is characterized by conglomerate formations and includes parts of the Collegats-Queralt Protected Natural Area. The Noguera Pallaresa river and Sant Antoni reservoir are also prominent natural features. Best hiking trails around Conca De Dalt The most popular hiking route is Montsor from La Pobla Segur — circular by the Alto Lo Tossalet, a 11.7 miles (18.9 km) trail that takes 6 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Claverol Trail, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.5 km) path. This route provides views of the local landscape and is suitable for those seeking a less strenuous option. Local hikers also love the Sant Pere de Vilanoveta from Aramunt — circular through the Bosque de Sant Corneli, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail leading through the Bosque de Sant Corneli, often completed in about 5 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Conca De Dalt is defined by mountainous terrain, deep ravines, and protected natural areas, with routes often passing through historic villages. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging high-mountain treks. The routes in Conca De Dalt are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Conca De Dalt's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find in Conca De Dalt?
A: Conca De Dalt offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from leisurely walks to challenging high-mountain treks. You'll find routes through mountainous terrain, deep ravines, and protected natural areas, often passing through historic villages. There are approximately 82 routes in total, with 15 easy, 53 moderate, and 14 difficult options.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Conca De Dalt has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219793097/sant-corneli-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Corneli Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 4 km long with minimal elevation gain, offering a pleasant walk.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Conca De Dalt?
A: The region is characterized by a mountainous environment with significant elevation changes, pronounced ravines, and dramatic cliffs, including the impressive Collegats Canyon. You'll also encounter the Noguera Pallaresa river and the Sant Antoni reservoir, as well as parts of the Collegats-Queralt Protected Natural Area, offering diverse flora, fauna, and birdlife.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Conca De Dalt?
A: Yes, many routes in Conca De Dalt are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example of a challenging circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1220085552/montsor-from-la-pobla-de-segur-circular-by-the-alto-lo-tossalet" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montsor from La Pobla Segur — circular by the Alto Lo Tossalet&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 19 km.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore along the trails?
A: Many hiking routes in Conca De Dalt pass through the municipality's twelve small villages, which retain their medieval charm. You can discover ancient buildings, churches, hermitages, and castle ruins, such as the castle of Aramunt and Aramunt Vell with its Civil War trenches and bunkers, adding a cultural dimension to your hike.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Conca De Dalt?
A: The routes in Conca De Dalt are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the integration of historical sites, and the stunning natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots worth visiting?
A: The region offers numerous scenic spots. For panoramic views, the Moixeró peak provides breathtaking vistas of the plain, the Cadí mountain range, and Pedraforca. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5732929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sant Antoni Reservoir and Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the presence of the Noguera Pallaresa river and the Sant Antoni reservoir means many trails offer views of or pass by water bodies. Notable lakes and reservoirs in the broader area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montcortés&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2673111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Antoni Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3955011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basturs Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trails in Conca De Dalt?
A: While direct public transport to every trailhead might be limited, some routes, like those departing from La Pobla de Segur, are accessible via public transport to larger towns. From these towns, you may need to arrange local transport or start your hike directly.

Q: What is a good moderate-difficulty trail to experience the local landscape?
A: For a moderate challenge that offers good views of the local landscape, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1220135840/claverol-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Claverol Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This path is about 10.5 km long and is suitable for those seeking a less strenuous option than the most difficult routes, but still offers a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options in the Conca De Dalt area?
A: Yes, the region is part of initiatives that have developed stage-based hiking routes. You can find long-distance routes (GR) that span many hours or days, such as parts of the 'Eastern Ramat' which explores Collegats Canyon and sections of the Alt Pirineu Natural Park.
tips: Climb up the valley to see the ruins of the Castle of Sant Pere de Vilanoveta. The views towards the lake are spectacular on a clear day. Historical ruins from the dark civil war with bunkers and trenches.
Walk to Aramunt for its history and for a spectacular view of the valley.

waypoints.name: Ruins of Sant Pere de Vilanoveta Castle
